stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: ZoomInfo shares surged as the go-to-market data platform continued its recovery rally. The stock has been climbing from multi-year lows after analysts upgraded the name on improved B2B software spending trends and stabilizing net retention rates heading into the second half of 2026.

stocks.name: Intuitive Machines
stocks.explanation: Intuitive Machines extended its recent decline after announcing a $500 million at-the-market equity offering and losing key NASA lunar rover contracts to competitors Astrolab and Lunar Outpost. The SpaceX IPO also triggered rotation out of smaller space names as investors reallocated capital to the sector's new mega-cap.

stocks.name: Amkor Technology
stocks.explanation: Amkor Technology hit a fresh all-time high after a record Q1 earnings beat (EPS of $0.33 vs. $0.24 consensus), its first Investor Day in nearly 20 years, and confirmation of a major AMD packaging program win supporting the company's Arizona expansion thesis.

stocks.name: Lumentum Holdings
stocks.explanation: Lumentum pulled back as investors took profits on one of 2026's best-performing stocks, up roughly 140% year-to-date. Despite record revenue and soaring margins on its latest earnings call, the company confirmed supply constraints exceeding 30% for key photonics products, fueling debate about its ability to capitalize on AI networking demand.

stocks.name: Rigetti Computing
stocks.explanation: Rigetti Computing fell as quantum computing stocks broadly sold off following Quantinuum's NASDAQ IPO, which raised $1.68 billion and siphoned investor capital from smaller quantum names. The sector rotation reflects a flight to the segment's perceived quality leader.

stocks.name: Quantum Computing Inc.
stocks.explanation: Quantum Computing Inc. dropped alongside the broader quantum sector selloff triggered by Quantinuum's June IPO. The stock remains highly volatile and speculative, with investors rotating capital toward the newly public Quantinuum as the sector's institutional-grade option.

stocks.name: IonQ Inc.
stocks.explanation: IonQ declined in sympathy with the quantum computing sector pullback following Quantinuum's IPO listing. Despite IonQ's stronger fundamental position including government contracts and enterprise partnerships, the sector-wide rotation weighed on shares.

stocks.name: SoundHound AI
stocks.explanation: SoundHound AI continued its slide, now down over 30% year-to-date, as legal scrutiny intensified with multiple shareholder litigation firms probing governance and acquisition accounting practices. A $300 million at-the-market offering added dilution concerns to the already negative sentiment around profitability.

stocks.name: Western Digital
stocks.explanation: Western Digital surged after Morgan Stanley projected HDD demand growth of 40-50% annually through 2028 versus 30-35% supply growth, lifting 2028 EPS estimates 70% above consensus. Bank of America reinforced the AI infrastructure storage demand thesis, propelling the stock past $700 for the first time.

stocks.name: Snap Inc.
stocks.explanation: Snap shares continued their 2026 decline, now down over 40% year-to-date, after multiple analyst downgrades from Citi, RBC, and JPMorgan. The collapse of its Perplexity AI partnership, cautious revenue guidance, ongoing unprofitability, and an EU child-safety investigation have eroded investor confidence.

stocks.name: Ciena Corporation
stocks.explanation: Ciena pulled back amid broader networking equipment profit-taking after a strong run on AI data center buildout expectations. The optical networking leader has been volatile as investors debate whether near-term order growth can sustain the elevated multiple.

stocks.name: AST SpaceMobile
stocks.explanation: AST SpaceMobile shares declined as the satellite-to-cellphone company faced continued skepticism about its commercial launch timeline and capital needs. The stock has been pressured by the SpaceX IPO redirecting space-sector investment flows.

stocks.name: Lucid Group
stocks.explanation: Lucid Group dropped as the luxury EV maker continues to face headwinds from slowing EV demand growth and elevated cash burn. The stock remains under pressure from competition and questions about the company's path to profitability despite Saudi backing.

stocks.name: Corning Inc.
stocks.explanation: Corning retreated from recent highs after its strong run on AI-driven fiber optics demand. Investors took profits despite the company's bullish outlook for its optical communications segment, which has benefited from hyperscaler data center expansions.

stocks.name: Coherent Corp.
stocks.explanation: Coherent Corp. declined alongside other optical and photonics names in a sector-wide pullback. Despite strong demand for its AI-related transceivers and datacom products, the stock gave back gains as investors rotated out of the high-flying optical networking trade.
